Details: Removal of aldicarb with reverse osmosis (RO) membrane was very effective in two studies (equal or greater than 98 percent removal) [1487, 1488]. The membrane units tested were components of two point-of-use drinking water treatment systems, which were Watts Premier WP-4V and Ecowater Systems ERO-R450E. The RO membrane of each system was tested alone for aldicarb removal. The RO influent concentrations were 1,000 μg/L and 1,100 μg/L in the two studies. The influent pH levels were in the range of 7.0 to 8.0 in the two studies.
